themes: Mac mini as solo-founder compute platform

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: 473 replies to Greg Brockman asking what people are building with Codex — the thread is a live snapshot of real agent use cases worth mining for product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick argues AI agents can now independently reconstruct complex academic papers — a concrete capability milestone that signals enterprise adoption friction is eroding faster than institutions realize.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Casual but high-engagement observation that GPT-5.5 feels 'more serious, less chatty' — early qualitative signal of a tone/persona shift that matters for sales and enterprise positioning.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: DeepSeek V4 open-source at 1.6T parameters with 1M context vs. OpenAI's $200/month paywall is the clearest single framing of the pricing disruption story circulating right now.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: GLM-5.1 on BytePlus at ~5x lower cost than Anthropic, with 8-hour autonomous task loops natively in Cursor and Claude Code — relevant to anyone evaluating model cost in agentic pipelines.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Brynjolfsson's 25M payroll record study showing entry-level AI-exposed jobs down 13% is the hard data counterpoint to the LinkedIn job-posting optimism narrative.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Mac mini 32GB+ on 10-18 week backorder with PMs buying them as personal AI compute boxes — the OpenClaw pattern (dedicated local agent box) is a concrete distribution and solo-builder infrastructure signal.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Mike Krieger leaving his Figma board seat on the exact day The Information broke the Anthropic design tool story is the sharpest competitive-intelligence read on Opus 4.7 vs. Figma this week.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: AI-generated App Store screenshots in seconds via Anything — a low-friction distribution tool directly relevant to indie dev launch workflows.

voices.why: Consistent early takes on model cost competition, Anthropic PR dynamics, and user-sentiment shifts — punches above his follower count on strategic signal.
Wharton prof translating AI capability milestones into institutional adoption friction — relevant for enterprise sales and product strategy framing.
Rare practitioner-level signal from inside OpenAI; his Codex reply thread is a primary source on real agentic use cases.

examples: RoundtableSpace all-caps hype posts recycling the same Claude/GPT demos with no new insight
JaynitMakwana GlobalGPT promotional spam thread (fake model version numbers, affiliate links)
aakashgupta pop-science trivia feed (jaw evolution, cat frequencies, Stallone body fat, Domino's pizza)

fading.theme: agent-native PM role & system design bar
